mnemonics.title: AI Categories by Capability
mnemonics.content: N-A-S: Narrow (specific tasks), General (human-level), Super (beyond human) - Think 'NAGS' like someone nagging you to remember!

mnemonics.title: Turing Test Components
mnemonics.content: J-H-M: Judge, Human, Machine - Remember as 'Just Have Mind' - the judge tests if the machine has a mind!

mnemonics.title: Strong vs Weak AI
mnemonics.content: S-W: Strong = Real Mind, Weak = Simulation - 'S' for Substance (real understanding), 'W' for 'as if' (simulation only)

acronyms.usage: Current AI specialized in single tasks like spam filters, voice assistants, chess programs
Hypothetical AI with human-level intelligence across all cognitive domains (not yet achieved)
Theoretical AI surpassing human intelligence in virtually every aspect

acronyms.expansion: Completely Automated Public Turing Test to Tell Computers and Humans Apart
acronyms.usage: Used on websites to distinguish human users from bots
Still theoretical - would be able to perform any intellectual task a human can do

acronyms.expansion: Highest award in computer science, named after Alan Turing
acronyms.usage: Often called the 'Nobel Prize of Computing', first awarded in 1966

visualTips.visualization: Imagine three rooms: Judge in Room 1 types questions → Messages go to both Room 2 (Human) and Room 3 (Machine) → Both type responses → If judge can't tell who is who, Machine passes!
Draw a horizontal line from left to right: ANI (single point) → AGI (middle plateau) → ASI (highest peak going beyond chart) - like climbing a mountain!

Picture a person in a small room with rule books, passing Chinese papers in and out through a slot - they manipulate symbols perfectly but understand nothing - like a bookkeeper who processes invoices without knowing what the company does!

analogies.analogy: A calculator - extremely good at math but can't do anything else. It's like a hammer: great for nails, useless for screws.
A human adult - can learn anything, drive a car, write poetry, solve new problems. Like a versatile toolbox with every tool imaginable.
Imagine a genius whose IQ is not just higher than Einstein's but beyond all human comprehension combined - like comparing a cat's intelligence to a human's, but reversed.
A foreign exchange student who memorizes all exam answers perfectly but doesn't understand the language - they get perfect scores but zero comprehension!
It's like playing '20 Questions' blindfolded with two people - if you can't tell which is the computer, it passes! Like a voice masquerade ball.
